jokes.text: Carol, they're towing your car! Damn it. My kids are in there.
jokes.setup: Someone's car is being towed - a normal inconvenience
jokes.punchline: The absurd revelation that children were left in the car
jokes.explanation: Perfect Spaceman absurdity - treating child endangerment with casual annoyance. Great character integration and economy.

jokes.text: I came all the way up to Westchester just to avoid you. This is Westchester? I am lost.
jokes.setup: Someone drove somewhere to avoid another person
jokes.punchline: They accidentally ended up in the exact place they were avoiding
jokes.explanation: Solid ironic reversal with good timing and efficiency.

jokes.text: If a man takes it, he won't grow breasts, although that would be awesome.
jokes.setup: Doctor explaining that progesterone won't cause breast growth in men
jokes.punchline: His immediate follow-up that breast growth would be awesome
jokes.explanation: Perfect Spaceman moment - medical authority undermined by bizarre personal preference. Excellent character integration.

jokes.text: No money for the whorehouse? That's fine. I'll just look down while hiding my crotch with an oriental fan.
jokes.setup: Context suggests this is a random, inappropriate interjection
jokes.punchline: The incredibly specific and bizarre solution to an unstated problem
jokes.explanation: Peak Spaceman randomness - completely out of nowhere but perfectly in character. Highly quotable absurdity.

jokes.text: Hey, that's a series wrap on Leo Spaceman, suckers. Bye.
jokes.setup: Spaceman being recruited as Surgeon General
jokes.punchline: Breaking the fourth wall to announce his character is leaving the show
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke acknowledging the character write-off. Perfect economy and self-awareness typical of 30 Rock's meta-humor.

jokes.text: Liz Lemon, that stuff will shrink your testicles, but there are bad side effects as well.
jokes.setup: Warning about side effects of fertility drugs
jokes.punchline: Implying testicle shrinkage is the good side effect
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - sets up expectation that testicle shrinkage is bad, then reveals Tracy thinks it's actually good.

jokes.text: Now, I'm not a woman, so of course I can say whatever I want.
jokes.setup: Tracy about to give opinion on fertility treatments
jokes.punchline: His casual acknowledgment that being male gives him license to be insensitive
jokes.explanation: Sharp social commentary delivered with Tracy's characteristic obliviousness. Efficient and character-specific.

jokes.text: I got octavia Spencer to play the lead... Harriet something.
jokes.setup: Tracy casting his Harriet Tubman movie
jokes.punchline: Not remembering Harriet Tubman's last name despite making a movie about her
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy ignorance - treating a historical icon with casual disregard. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: You know, I once played Frederick douglass in a one-woman show that the university of Maryland Diamondback called 'too confusing to be offensive.'
jokes.setup: Tracy relating to Harriet Tubman casting
jokes.punchline: The absurd image of Tracy in a one-woman show as Frederick Douglass, plus the perfect review quote
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd concept with perfect fake review quote. The phrase 'too confusing to be offensive' is comedy gold and highly quotable.

jokes.text: Exactly. No Tracy.
jokes.setup: Liz saying 'No, Tracy' meaning 'No, you can't skip rehearsal'
jokes.punchline: Tracy interpreting it as agreement that there will be 'No Tracy' at rehearsal
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay misunderstanding. Maximum economy - entire joke in two words.

jokes.text: She wanted to be cremated, and she ended up dying in a fire. Such a considerate woman.
jokes.setup: Hank remembering his mother's death wishes
jokes.punchline: Calling her 'considerate' for accidentally fulfilling her cremation wish by dying in a fire
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor - finding the silver lining in a horrific death. Perfect deadpan delivery implied by context.

jokes.text: One... birthday boy always sleeps in the largest tiger cage.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining his strict birthday rules
jokes.punchline: The completely insane first rule involving tiger cages
jokes.explanation: Completely unexpected absurdity. The casual mention of tiger cages as if it's normal is peak 30 Rock humor.

jokes.text: Some of the rules may be specific to my time in Vietnam.
jokes.setup: Jack's bizarre birthday rule about tiger cages
jokes.punchline: Casual acknowledgment that his birthday rules might be Vietnam-specific
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan follow-up that makes the absurd even more absurd. Great callback to Jack's unexplained Vietnam history.

jokes.text: Hank Jr. is writing a children's book about a giraffe who learns he doesn't have to work hard.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining why Hank's kids aren't suitable for business
jokes.punchline: The hilariously anti-motivational children's book concept
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of typical children's book morals. The anti-work ethic message is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: That's the same age I was when I started Kabletown, got married, and had my first white child.
jokes.setup: Hank explaining Kaylie will be 21 after college
jokes.punchline: The casual specification of 'first white child'
jokes.explanation: Dark humor revealing character's problematic past. The casual racism is perfectly in character for the old executive.

jokes.text: What? Is this guy trying to tank the company? But, boy, you proved me wrong.
jokes.setup: Hank praising Jack's work at NBC
jokes.punchline: Admitting he initially thought Jack was trying to destroy the company
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke about NBC's actual struggles. Solid ironic reversal with good structure.

jokes.text: Celebrity homonym is the number-one show in America.
jokes.setup: Hank praising Jack's successful shows
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous concept of a celebrity word-game show
jokes.explanation: Clever fake show concept playing on word confusion. Good wordplay but not exceptional.

jokes.text: The word is 'racket.' Okay, 'racket,' like a tennis racket. Sorry. It's the other one. Again?
jokes.setup: Demonstration of the Celebrity Homonym show
jokes.punchline: Contestant repeatedly failing to understand which meaning of 'racket'
jokes.explanation: Solid escalating confusion gag. Shows the absurd game show in action but not particularly innovative.

jokes.text: It'll work great, just like Conan and Jay.
jokes.setup: Hank explaining how Jack and Kaylie will work together
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to the notoriously disastrous Conan/Leno situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect meta-reference to NBC's real Tonight Show disaster. Maximum economy and insider knowledge.

jokes.text: Have you ever updated your flash player? It is so sad. What happens to version 11.4?
jokes.setup: Liz crying from hormones
jokes.punchline: Getting emotional about outdated software versions
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific absurdity. Only Liz would cry about software updates. Perfect character-driven humor.

jokes.text: I'm taking hormone shots to have a baby, you son of a bitch! A beautiful little baby.
jokes.setup: Liz emotional from hormones
jokes.punchline: Aggressively calling Jack 'son of a bitch' while talking about wanting a baby
jokes.explanation: Good juxtaposition of aggression and maternal desire. Solid character moment showing hormone effects.

jokes.text: Being a woman is the worst.
jokes.setup: Liz dealing with hormone-induced emotions
jokes.punchline: Her blunt assessment of womanhood
jokes.explanation: Classic Liz deadpan observation. Maximum economy and character consistency.

jokes.text: Just like poor Nixon. He did good things in China.
jokes.setup: Liz crying about Jack potentially resigning in shame
jokes.punchline: Defending Nixon while hormone-emotional
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz moment - finding the weirdest historical parallel while emotional. Great character specificity.

jokes.text: Octavia, excellent, you're black.
jokes.setup: Tracy greeting his lead actress
jokes.punchline: Commenting on her race as if it's a pleasant surprise
jokes.explanation: Perfectly uncomfortable Tracy moment. Character-specific obliviousness creates cringe humor.

jokes.text: Well, you're just gonna have to rewrite the script and explain how my character can go through walls.
jokes.setup: Octavia refusing to use the door in the script
jokes.punchline: Demanding impossible wall-phasing ability instead of using the door
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of unreasonable demands. Absurd but logical within the character's stubbornness.

jokes.text: But slaves didn't wear t-shirts. That sounds like a Tracy problem, Tracy.
jokes.setup: Octavia wanting to wear a promotional t-shirt in the slave movie
jokes.punchline: Dismissing historical accuracy as 'Tracy's problem'
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamics. Shows Octavia adopting Tracy's own dismissive attitude against him.

jokes.text: I got to go home and feed my eels. They're not electric, but I have a plan.
jokes.setup: Octavia leaving early from filming
jokes.punchline: The bizarre eel situation and ominous 'plan' to electrify them
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly weird escalation. The casual mention of electrification plans is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Boy, Jay-Z and Shakespeare have nothing in common. Or do they?
jokes.setup: Len undercover as a drama teacher
jokes.punchline: The hilariously bad attempt at profound teaching
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Len's terrible undercover work. Generic 'deep thoughts' perfectly capture bad teaching.

jokes.text: Jan Foster is currently in a 'lesbian relationship' with Ms. Siegal from the math department.
jokes.setup: Len explaining his undercover identity
jokes.punchline: The unnecessarily elaborate fake lesbian relationship
jokes.explanation: Good absurd detail about the undercover operation. The air quotes around 'lesbian relationship' add to the humor.

jokes.text: Pedal faster, fatties. God, I love how much you disgust me.
jokes.setup: Banks teaching spin class
jokes.punchline: His gleeful sadism toward his students
jokes.explanation: Perfect Banks character moment. The sadistic pleasure is exactly what we'd expect from him.

jokes.text: You're the gay one. Wanting to be with a woman... How gay is that? You win sex against a man, that's as straight as it gets.
jokes.setup: Jack calling Banks' spin class a gay cliche
jokes.punchline: Banks' twisted logic about heterosexuality being gay
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd logic delivered with complete confidence. The phrase 'win sex against a man' is comedy gold and highly quotable.

jokes.text: Remember A-8? You peeked.
jokes.setup: Jack referencing their past battleship game
jokes.punchline: Accusing Banks of cheating at a children's game decades later
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing how their rivalry extends to childhood games. Maximum economy.

jokes.text: An assload.
jokes.setup: Jack asking how much time they've wasted sparring
jokes.punchline: Banks' crude but accurate assessment
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan response. The vulgarity contrasts nicely with Jack's more formal phrasing.

jokes.text: Gregory Linens teamed up with Thomas n'things.
jokes.setup: Jack comparing potential partnerships
jokes.punchline: Fake business duo names based on store puns
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay combining Linens 'n Things store name into fake business partners. Moderately funny pun.

jokes.text: Gay enough that Kaylie's parents have never actually had heterosexual intercourse.
jokes.setup: Jack asking how gay Hank's son is
jokes.punchline: The extreme revelation about the marriage
jokes.explanation: Good plot revelation with comedic impact. The clinical phrasing adds to the humor.

jokes.text: Very handsome, horrible personality... I'm totally in love with him.
jokes.setup: Banks describing Trevor Poole
jokes.punchline: Admitting attraction despite the horrible personality
jokes.explanation: Perfect Banks moment showing his shallow but honest dating preferences. Good character insight.

jokes.text: You've moved from the 'well-meaning lesbian' pile to 'found a man, comma, living a lie.'
jokes.setup: Bev explaining Liz's new adoption category
jokes.punchline: The cynical categorization system with brutal honesty
jokes.explanation: Brilliant characterization of adoption agency cynicism. The 'comma, living a lie' detail is perfectly brutal.

jokes.text: I can give you a six-year-old yesterday. Pick a color.
jokes.setup: Bev offering older children for adoption
jokes.punchline: Treating children like products with casual racism
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about adoption process bureaucracy. Maximum economy but edgy territory.

jokes.text: And I always pictured myself getting double-teamed by two the rocks. But sometimes we have to make compromises.
jokes.setup: Liz saying she pictured getting a newborn
jokes.punchline: Bev's completely inappropriate sexual fantasy comparison
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly inappropriate response. The specificity of 'two the rocks' and treating it like normal advice is perfect.

jokes.setup: Liz asking how old is too old for a woman to have a baby
jokes.punchline: Jack's absurdly young age limit
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack moment - maximum economy with one word revealing his absurd worldview. Excellent deadpan delivery implied.

jokes.text: Steve Forbes was born a ball of fingers. He had the best surgeons money can buy, but you can still tell.
jokes.setup: Jack discussing babies born with defects
jokes.punchline: Claiming Steve Forbes was literally born as a ball of fingers
jokes.explanation: Absolutely brilliant absurd imagery. The phrase 'ball of fingers' is instant comedy gold, and 'but you can still tell' is perfect.

jokes.text: For every orphan Annie, there's a 30-year-old Russian dwarf who's just pretending to be a child, according to a movie that I watched part of.
jokes.setup: Liz explaining why older adoptions are risky
jokes.punchline: The bizarre movie reference and casual admission of incomplete viewing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz logic - basing life decisions on half-remembered movies. The specific scenario is delightfully absurd.

jokes.text: The reason being a week-long party in Stephen sondheim's sex dungeon. The workmanship down there is exquisite, but it took forever.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining why Banks has information
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of Sondheim's sex dungeon and critiquing the renovation quality
jokes.explanation: Absolutely brilliant absurd reference. The casual mention plus renovation critique is peak 30 Rock absurdity.

jokes.text: What's happening to your left blob?
jokes.setup: Banks noticing Liz's hormone-affected appearance
jokes.punchline: Refers to her breast as 'left blob'
jokes.explanation: Effective cringe moment - Banks' crude observation about hormone effects

jokes.text: Oh, renovations in New York.
jokes.setup: Jack's insane story about Sondheim's sex dungeon renovations
jokes.punchline: Liz's completely understated reaction focusing on the renovation aspect
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz reaction - missing the insane parts and focusing on the mundane. Brilliant deadpan response.

jokes.text: Thanks for the compliment, Lemon. Train travel's for hoboes.
jokes.setup: Liz saying Jack is 'off the rails'
jokes.punchline: Jack taking it as a compliment because he hates trains
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection showing Jack's elitist worldview. Perfect character moment with good economy.

jokes.text: The only backstabbing I do now is... Well, you know.
jokes.setup: Banks claiming he's changed from backstabbing
jokes.punchline: The obvious sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Predictable but character-appropriate sexual humor. Good for Banks but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: I would have been a Nazi. Who do we know who's immature and vicious enough to...
jokes.setup: Liz explaining she'll do anything for approval
jokes.punchline: Casually admitting she would have been a Nazi
jokes.explanation: Dark but perfectly Liz self-awareness. The casual admission is both horrifying and funny.

jokes.text: Let's destroy her.
jokes.setup: Jack and Liz looking for someone vicious to help them
jokes.punchline: Jenna's immediate enthusiasm for destruction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna entrance - immediately ready for chaos without context. Maximum economy and character consistency.

jokes.text: Sound like she acting like you. But I'm impossible to deal with. No one has ever been able to rein in my hilarious antics.
jokes.setup: Grizz pointing out Octavia is acting like Tracy
jokes.punchline: Tracy's complete lack of self-awareness about being manageable
jokes.explanation: Great character irony - Tracy not realizing Liz manages him daily. Good setup for his realization.

jokes.text: W.W.R.X.W. What would Liz Lemon do?
jokes.setup: Tracy deciding to act like Liz
jokes.punchline: Creating an unnecessarily complex acronym
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy logic - overcomplicating a simple concept. The extra letters add to the absurdity.

jokes.text: Jack, pleasure's the name of a pony I hate.
jokes.setup: Jack saying 'to what do I owe the pleasure'
jokes.punchline: Kaylie's bizarre reason for rejecting the phrase
jokes.explanation: Brilliant unexpected response. The specificity of hating a particular pony is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Oh, her brain's like silly putty, a toy I am too old for.
jokes.setup: Kaylie describing manipulating Jenna
jokes.punchline: The casual admission of being too old for toys
jokes.explanation: Good character detail showing Kaylie's maturity mixed with teenage concerns. Solid metaphor.

jokes.text: I use gadzorp.
jokes.setup: Kaylie explaining she doesn't keep things on her phone
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous made-up technology name
jokes.explanation: Good fake tech name that sounds appropriately ridiculous. Shows the generation gap effectively.

jokes.text: I might as well be looking at Justin Timberlake.
jokes.setup: Kaylie calling Jack old and out of touch
jokes.punchline: Using Justin Timberlake as the example of old
jokes.explanation: Good generational humor - someone still relatively young being considered ancient by teenagers.

jokes.text: I quit, cows. Tell your husbands I tried.
jokes.setup: Octavia leaving the DNA testing situation
jokes.punchline: Her dramatic exit calling everyone cows
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic exit with the insult and marriage assumption. Character-appropriate diva behavior.

jokes.text: Blargh. I'm Liz Lemon. I'm in charge! Nerds! I want to have a baby. My boyfriend is a pilot or something. Sandwiches.
jokes.setup: Tracy trying to imitate Liz Lemon
jokes.punchline: His hilariously reductive impression hitting all her key traits
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character impression that's both accurate and insulting. The word 'Sandwiches' as a complete summary is perfect.

jokes.text: I'm a Jedi.
jokes.setup: Octavia trying to out-crazy Tracy
jokes.punchline: Her random Star Wars claim
jokes.explanation: Weak escalation attempt. Random doesn't equal funny here - lacks the specificity that makes Tracy's absurdity work.

jokes.text: I think my greatest disguise was len wazniak.
jokes.setup: Len deciding to stay as Jan Foster
jokes.punchline: Suggesting his regular identity was also a disguise
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke suggesting the character we know isn't real. Deep philosophical humor about identity.

jokes.text: Hi honey! It's 20% of your midterm grade.
jokes.setup: Jan Foster appearing at the party
jokes.punchline: Treating the spy mission like a school assignment
jokes.explanation: Good commitment to the teacher character even in spy mode. Shows Len's dedication to the role.

jokes.text: engaged to Ms. Siegal.
jokes.setup: Len explaining his cover identity
jokes.punchline: The fake relationship has escalated to engagement
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the fake relationship subplot. Shows how deep undercover Len has gone.

jokes.text: that I wouldn't notice miss Foster sit on her testicles the other day
jokes.setup: Kaylie explaining how she knew it was a setup
jokes.punchline: Len's poor drag performance including anatomical mishaps
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual comedy detail. The casual mention of this mishap is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: So Jenna is an Ashkenazi Jew with an extra 'Y' chromosome?
jokes.setup: Learning the DNA was Jenna's
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous genetic results
jokes.explanation: Good absurd genetic results that somehow make sense for Jenna. The XYY detail adds scientific absurdity.

jokes.text: We were all talking about it before at the mall.
jokes.setup: Kaylie explaining everyone hates Jack
jokes.punchline: The teenage setting of 'at the mall'
jokes.explanation: Good character detail showing Kaylie's teenage world. The mall setting undercuts the serious plotting.

jokes.text: Yes, I suppose that's one way this could have played out.
jokes.setup: Kaylie thinking she's won
jokes.punchline: Jack's casual dismissal suggesting another plan
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack confidence. Sets up the counter-reversal with characteristic understatement.

jokes.text: A birthday card.
jokes.setup: Kaylie asking what Jack sent to Hank
jokes.punchline: The anticlimactic but devastating simple solution
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection. After all the complex scheming, the solution is beautifully simple. Perfect economy and structure.

jokes.text: While I distracted you, you forgot Pop-Pop's 70th birthday.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining his real strategy
jokes.punchline: The simple but devastating oversight
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the birthday setup. Shows Jack's superior strategic thinking while using established character traits.

jokes.text: After college, I'm gonna have to go into publishing and... Marry a finance guy and... Do charity stuff.
jokes.setup: Kaylie realizing her defeat
jokes.punchline: Her life will be stereotypical wealthy woman trajectory
jokes.explanation: Good social satire - the predetermined path of wealthy women spelled out matter-of-factly

jokes.text: After college, I'm gonna have to go into publishing and... Marry a finance guy and... Do charity stuff.
jokes.setup: Kaylie realizing she's lost
jokes.punchline: Her horrifying vision of upper-class female life
jokes.explanation: Good social satire about privileged women's limited ambitions. The progression from finance guy to charity is perfect.

jokes.text: That didn't need to happen. That was just mean.
jokes.setup: Jack having Banks mail the birthday card unnecessarily
jokes.punchline: Banks calling out Jack's gratuitous cruelty
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing even Banks has limits. The deadpan moral judgment is perfect.

jokes.text: And where did you get that sweater? Is that from chico's harvest naturals collection?
jokes.setup: Liz noticing Tracy's attempt to dress like her
jokes.punchline: Specific knowledge of terrible clothing brand
jokes.explanation: Good character detail - Liz's specific knowledge of awful clothing brands fits perfectly

jokes.text: No, they shattered in a urinal.
jokes.setup: Liz asking if Tracy popped the lenses out of her glasses
jokes.punchline: The disgusting explanation for the missing lenses
jokes.explanation: Good gross-out humor that's character-appropriate for Tracy. The casualness makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Like my booger-eating? Please, let me just have one. I need it, baby.
jokes.setup: Tracy listing his horrible habits Liz helped him overcome
jokes.punchline: The disgusting habit and desperate plea
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor showing Tracy's childlike behavior. The addiction-like pleading adds to the discomfort.

jokes.text: Hey, those lego men were on a rescue mission to save the other lego men that I swallowed.
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining why Liz had to stay up with him when sick
jokes.punchline: The insane logic behind eating Lego figures
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Tracy logic - creating an elaborate rescue narrative for his dangerous behavior. Peak absurdist character comedy.

jokes.text: Bitch, more than one person works here.
jokes.setup: Liz calling back about adoption
jokes.punchline: Bev's hostile response to being expected to remember her
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bev hostility. The casual profanity and complete dismissal of Liz's expectations is character gold.

jokes.text: We went beyond.
jokes.setup: Discussion of where they went last night - Bed Bath & Beyond
jokes.punchline: The literal interpretation of going 'beyond'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on the store name. Grizz's deadpan delivery makes the simple pun work well.
